task_completion.description: Create a new reference item of type 'E-mail'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new reference item is visible in the reference manager application, and its 'Item Type' is explicitly set to 'E-mail'.

task_completion.description: Set the subject of the email reference
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Subject' or 'Title' field in the item details pane is filled with the text 'Resignation implications'.

task_completion.description: Set the author of the email
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Author' field in the item details pane is filled with 'Skilling' for the last name and 'Jeffrey' for the first name.

task_completion.description: Set the recipient of the email
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Recipient' field in the item details pane is filled with 'Lay' for the last name and 'Kenneth' for the first name.

task_completion.description: Set the date of the email
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Date' field in the item details pane is filled with '2001-08-14'.

task_completion.description: Set the abstract of the email
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Abstract' field in the item details pane contains the text 'Discussion regarding the public announcement of resignation and stock impact.'

integrity.description: Agent used the graphical user interface to create the entry
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the reference manager application open with the item being edited manually in the UI, rather than terminal commands executing database inserts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with a genuine reference management application
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI belongs to a reference manager (such as Zotero) and not a generic text editor (like Notepad or Gedit) where the agent simply typed out the key-value pairs.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal scripts to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing Python scripts or sqlite3 commands modifying the reference manager's underlying database directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Jeffrey Skilling (Author) resigned as Enron CEO on August 14, 2001 (Date), notifying Kenneth Lay (Recipient). The resignation caused a significant stock impact (Abstract fragment).
